What do you think? …

fmerian
Tabstack by Mozilla

Tabstack by Mozilla [...] Atta 🐬
StartupBase

StartupBase

Apache 2.0 on both checkpoints is the real unlock here. Most frontier-level models stay closed exactly at the point where they become useful for production workloads.

Curious what fine-tuning looks like for teams that want to specialize it on a specific codebase or domain. Is that straightforward with the current weights?

FOREST TOP

Long-horizon coding needs evaluation beyond task completion. How do you measure recovery after bad edits, context drift across long runs, unnecessary tool calls, and whether a human can reconstruct why the agent made each decision?

Winsten Rasyada

The open weights are what caught my attention. Being able to run and tune a serious coding model on your own infrastructure is a big plus. [...] Anand Thakkar

The 256K context window at 23B active params is a strong architectural bet. Long-horizon agentic tasks without chunking is where most models fall apart. We've been navigating the inference infra tradeoff for our own agent layer, and self-hosted open weights changes the calculus significantly. How does Laguna handle attention at max context? Any sparse attention or positional tricks that keep it tractable?

Sanjay Kumar

Open weights with a 256K context window at 23B active params is a big deal for agentic coding, that should really help on long-horizon refactors where context runs out fast. Curious how Laguna M.1 holds up inside Cursor or Claude Code style loops. Congrats on shipping.

Atta 🐬
StartupBase

StartupBase

results.title: LLM Stats: Compare API models by benchmarks, cost & capabilities
results.content:
Now, we're working towards building the best semi-private, open and reproducible AI benchmarking community. We believe there's a greater need for independent benchmarks and environments that measure the progress of AI in areas like coding, science, visuals and long horizon tasks.

We're backed by Y Combinator and leaders of Hugging Face, Harvard Medical School, Daytona, Insight Data Science and many more.

Would love to hear your thoughts, see you on the platform.

Chris Hicken
Theysaid

Theysaid

@jonathanchavez This is exactly what the AI community needs right now! How do you plan to keep the benchmarks up-to-date as new models are released? It’d be great to see how you handle emerging models over time.

Jonathan Chávez
LLM Stats

LLM Stats

@chrishicken

Thanks Chris! [...] Lilou Lane

Really impressed by the vision here. Independent, reproducible benchmarks are critical for meaningful comparisons, and building a semi-private community adds a lot of trust. Looking forward to exploring the platform. [...] LLM Stats

results.content:
## Top reviewed foundation models

Top reviewed

Across the most-reviewed foundation models, the market splits between broad API platforms, open and portable stacks, and specialist reasoning or multimodal options. OpenAI stands out for end-to-end app building with tools, memory, voice, vision, and agents; Mistral AI emphasizes privacy, self-hosting, and multilingual enterprise workflows; DeepSeek competes on affordable coding, research, and long-document analysis."

results.title: mysite.ai: AI that builds your website & gets leads | Product Hunt
results.content:
Starting with websites, mysite.ai will soon handle social media, paid ads, lead gen, and content — adapting in real-time to your goals and audience. It’s not another SaaS tool. It’s an agent that acts.

We’re combining LLMs, vector memory, and long-horizon planning to create something that goes beyond suggestions — it executes. Our users describe it as “ChatGPT meets Wix meets CMO” — and they’re not wrong.

We just closed a €2.1M funding round to build the AI revenue engine for new businesses — with support from Inovo.vc and angels from Make.com, Alokai and others who’ve been in the trenches scaling products like this before.

We’re early — but already powering 20K+ users and climbing. We hit #1 on Product Hunt before, and we’re back with a better, faster, way more robust version. [...] mysite.ai

Thank you so much for the thoughtful feedback - really appreciate you taking the time.

Nirmoy Somaia
Patryk Pijanowski
mysite.ai

mysite.ai

Hey Nirmoy - really sorry about your experience 😔

You’re totally right - a bunch of things still don’t work properly. We built this MVP in just ~2 months and we’re fixing issues as fast as we can. The image library, svg handling, and layout bugs are already on our radar and getting addressed.

If you’ve got any other thoughts or annoyances, I’d honestly love to hear them - it really helps us build a better product.
